exam.level: 301 (applied practitioner) — community-reported; the official exam guide states a target candidate (solution architect, ~6+ months experience) but assigns no numeric level
exam.format: Scenario-based, multiple choice (single correct answer, no guessing penalty); delivery/proctoring is community-reported

mechanics_source.note: First-party = confirmed verbatim in the official 'Claude Certified Architect – Foundations Certification Exam Guide'. Community-reported = consistent across third-party prep sites (Panaversity, certsafari, open-exam-prep) but NOT in the official guide; treat as plausible defaults for simulation, not settled fact.
community_reported: fee_usd (99 — also conflicts with documented free early access for the first ~5,000 partner employees)

scenarios.note: First-party: the official guide says the exam uses scenario-based questions where each scenario frames a SET of questions, and that 4 scenarios are presented per sitting, picked at random from a fixed pool of 6. This trainer now mirrors that structure directly: data/scenarios.json holds 4 distinct instances of each of the 6 themes (24 scenarios); a sitting draws 4 themes at random and one random instance of each, presenting 4 scenarios × 15 linked questions = 60. Every scenario's 15 questions carry the SAME per-domain split (4/3/3/3/2 — the largest-remainder rounding of the 27/18/20/20/15 weights to 15), so any 4-of-6 sitting lands on the same weight-matched distribution (16/12/12/12/8). That is a faithful approximation: the exact weights would be 16.2/10.8/12/12/9, so the realized sitting runs ~+1 on Tool Design and ~-1 on Context Management — a one-question rounding effect, documented here.

scoring_model.note: First-party: results are reported as a scaled score of 100-1000 and the minimum passing score is 720 (per the official exam guide, which also notes scaled scoring equates scores across multiple exam forms, i.e. it is non-linear and form-dependent). This trainer uses a documented LINEAR approximation so the 720 pass line maps to ~70% correct; it will not match Anthropic's exact equated scaling.

domains.covers: Agentic loops, workflows vs. agents, prompt-chaining, routing, parallelization, orchestrator-worker, evaluator-optimizer, single- vs. multi-agent trade-offs, subagents and context isolation, session/state management, human-in-the-loop, the Agent SDK harness.

Tool definitions and input schemas, writing good tool descriptions, the tool_use/tool_result flow, parallel tool calls, forcing tool use, token-efficient tools, programmatic tool calling, MCP architecture (host/client/server), MCP primitives (tools/resources/prompts), transports (stdio vs. Streamable HTTP/SSE), the MCP connector, tool error handling and security.

name.fr: Configuration et workflows de Claude Code

domains.covers: CLAUDE.md memory hierarchy and imports, settings.json precedence and permissions, custom slash commands, hooks, project subagents, MCP configuration, headless/CI mode, plan mode, the Explore-Plan-Code-Commit workflow, GitHub Actions integration.

name.fr: Ingénierie des prompts et sorties structurées

domains.covers: Clear and direct instructions, multishot examples, chain-of-thought, XML tag structuring, system prompts, prefilling, output-format control, reliable JSON via tool use, temperature and stop sequences, prompt templates, evaluations and grading, hallucination mitigation.

Context-window budgeting, prompt caching (cache_control, breakpoints, TTL, prefix ordering), context editing and tool-result clearing, compaction/summarization, agentic memory, retries and exponential backoff, idempotency, rate limits and 429/529 handling, streaming, cost optimization.

session.sampling: A sitting draws 4 of the 6 scenario themes at random and one random instance of each; the 4 scenarios are laid out as contiguous blocks (the shared context stays pinned while you work that scenario's set) and questions are shuffled within each block. Because every scenario carries the 4/3/3/3/2 split, the realized per-domain counts are 16/12/12/12/8 = 60 on every sitting. Domains are ordered to match the official exam guide (Agentic, Tool/MCP, Claude Code, Prompt Eng., Context).

pool.rounding_rule: Pool: 6 themes × 4 instances × 15 questions = 360 (per-domain 96/72/72/72/48, each ≥ the 81/54/60/60/45 weighted targets). Per scenario: 4/3/3/3/2 — the largest-remainder rounding of 27/18/20/20/15 to 15. Sitting: 4 scenarios × 15 = 60 at 16/12/12/12/8.

sources_note: Verified June 2026 against first-party Anthropic sources, including the official 'Claude Certified Architect – Foundations Certification Exam Guide' (hosted on the Anthropic Academy / Skilljar course CDN) and the Claude Partner Network announcement (anthropic.com/news/claude-partner-network, Mar 12 2026). FIRST-PARTY CONFIRMED: exam name + provider, March 2026 launch, the $100M Partner Network program, scenario-based multiple-choice format (4 options, 1 correct, no guessing penalty), the 4-of-6 scenario structure, scaled scoring 100-1000, pass = 720, and the five domains with their exact weights (27/18/20/20/15). COMMUNITY-REPORTED (third-party only, NOT in the official guide): question count (60), time limit (120 min), proctoring, fee ($99), and the '301' level — kept here as reasonable simulation defaults but not authoritative. See resources/README.md for the full source manifest and access dates.
